mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window despite rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.5 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer demand, forcing corporate earnings downgrades and producing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based demand contraction, triggering negative earnings revisions and downward equity price pressure.
failure_condition: The joint decline condition fails to predict negative 20-day returns with classification accuracy above 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.5 percent month-over-month signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 1.5 percent coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with falling unemployment creates stagflationary pressure that compresses equity valuations and triggers negative forward returns as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive 20-day returns in greater than 50 percent of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent quarter-over-quarter indicates economic stagnation, reducing corporate profit expectations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 1.0 percent is followed by positive 60-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding employment and rising household income, supporting consumer spending and generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive payroll changes fail to predict positive 20-day returns with a statistically significant Rank IC above 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts anticipatory rate hikes, driving negative short-term equity returns as investors de-risk portfolios.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL acceleration coincides with positive 5-day returns in greater than 60 percent of instances.
